---
title: |
  Materials Genomics<br>Unit 5: Supervised Learning for Property Prediction
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Materials Genomics"
---

## 01. Title: Supervised Learning for Property Prediction
- Unit role in MG pipeline.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Build and evaluate supervised baselines.

## 03. Recap from Units 1–4
- Structure/data/FAIR dependencies.

## 04. Problem formulation
- Target, inputs, and decision context.

## 05. Regression vs classification in materials
- Typical target types.

## 06. Data matrix setup
- Samples, features, labels.

## 07. Feature sets for property prediction
- Composition, structure, process.

## 08. Target quality and uncertainty
- Noisy labels and confidence.

## 09. Split strategy for materials datasets
- Grouped and time-aware splits.

## 10. Leakage traps in materials ML
- Family overlap and proxies.

## 11. Baseline model principle
- Start simple, compare honestly.

## 12. Linear models
- Interpretability-first baseline.

## 13. Tree-based models
- Nonlinear interactions.

## 14. Kernel methods (conceptual)
- Flexible similarity modeling.

## 15. Ensemble models
- Variance reduction logic.

## 16. Hyperparameter tuning workflow
- Search and validation loops.

## 17. Metric selection for regression
- MAE, RMSE, R2 and meaning.

## 18. Metric selection for classification
- Precision, recall, F1, AUROC.

## 19. Calibration for probabilistic outputs
- Reliability of confidence.

## 20. Error analysis by subgroup
- Chemistry/structure slices.

## 21. Residual diagnostics
- Bias and heteroscedasticity.

## 22. Outlier handling strategy
- Data error vs novelty.

## 23. Imbalanced target handling
- Reweighting and resampling.

## 24. Multi-target prediction
- Joint property modeling overview.

## 25. Domain adaptation concern
- Different datasets/instruments.

## 26. Generalization across chemistries
- Extrapolation risk.

## 27. Uncertainty estimation preview
- Bridge to Unit 6.

## 28. Model comparison protocol
- Fair benchmark table.

## 29. Reproducibility checklist
- Seeds, versions, metadata.

## 30. Data provenance linkage
- Tracking source and transforms.

## 31. Case sketch: bandgap prediction
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 32. Case sketch: formation energy
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 33. Case sketch: stability class
- Classification scaffold.

## 34. Interpreting feature importance
- Caution with causal claims.

## 35. Partial dependence intuition
- Local/global effects.

## 36. SHAP-style interpretation preview
- Communication-focused view.

## 37. Decision thresholds in practice
- Utility-aware deployment.

## 38. Model cards for materials tasks
- Transparent reporting.

## 39. Common failure mode #1
- Overfitting to tiny datasets.

## 40. Common failure mode #2
- Validation leakage.

## 41. Common failure mode #3
- Ignoring uncertainty.

## 42. Mitigation checklist
- Robust workflow guardrails.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: define task
- Property + dataset + split.

## 44. Exercise scaffold: train baselines
- Two model families.

## 45. Exercise scaffold: evaluate metrics
- Multi-metric report.

## 46. Exercise scaffold: subgroup analysis
- At least one sliced diagnostic.

## 47. Exercise scaffold: error post-mortem
- Root-cause analysis.

## 48. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 49. Summary
- What to retain.

## 50. References + transition to Unit 6
- Uncertainty and error bars.

::: {#refs}
:::
